dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal requires trend confirmation (5 bars minimum) and 1% move beyond swing point, but these concepts cannot be directly expressed with allowed indicators. Used sma_5 crossover as proxy for trend establishment and price movement beyond prior swing (prev_high/prev_low) as proxy for momentum confirmation. Confirmation bias filter (entry on breakout or next bar) is inherent to breakout logic. Entry direction is context-dependent based on trend direction. Exit uses sma_5 breach as trend reversal signal per conditions specified.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal requires trend confirmation (5 bars minimum) and 1% move beyond swing point, but these concepts cannot be directly expressed with allowed indicators. Used sma_5 crossover as proxy for trend establishment and price movement beyond prior swing (prev_high/prev_low) as proxy for momentum confirmation. Confirmation bias filter (entry on breakout or next bar) is inherent to breakout logic. Entry direction is context-dependent based on trend direction. Exit uses sma_5 breach as trend reversal signal per conditions specified.

source_rule_text: A trader should enter positions aligned with the direction of price trends that form due to market underreaction and gradual catch-up to fundamental value, because confirmation bias causes investors to weight confirming evidence more heavily, perpetuating trend continuation.
